findings.title: Comma-bearing focus names make the compare gate fail open
findings.claim: Both implementations split focus values on every comma while allowing slice and boundary names containing commas. If rows named `A`, `B`, and `A,B` coexist, attempting to focus `A,B` actually focuses only `A` and `B`, allowing a regression in `A,B` to pass.
findings.reproduction: Constructed before/after slice reports with `A` and `B` fixed at 1%, while `A,B` increased from 10% to 20%. Both `.venv/bin/clankerprof compare ... --focus-slices 'A,B'` and `target/debug/clankerprof-rs compare ... --focus-slices 'A,B'` exited 0 and emitted `"has_regression": false`; the `A,B` row showed `"delta_abs": 10.0`, `"delta_rel": 100.0`, and `"status": "stable"`. Without focus, both exited 2 with `"has_regression": true` and `"status": "regression"`. The same false-green result reproduced with boundary reports and `--focus-boundaries`. Separately, both slice CLIs successfully produced a row named `A,B` from a slice definition with that name, proving it is valid producer output. Python splits at clankerprof/cli.py:263-269 and Rust at main.rs:1346-1355; neither name validator prohibits commas.

findings.title: Misspelled slice-config options are silently ignored
findings.claim: The YAML/TOML slice-options config has no top-level key allowlist. A typo can silently disable filtering or another analysis option while producing an ordinary successful report.
findings.reproduction: Used a valid one-sample facts artifact totaling 7ns and config `filterz:\n  - name:Never\n`. Both `.venv/bin/clankerprof slices --facts ... --config ...` and `target/debug/clankerprof-rs slices --facts ... --config ...` exited 0, emitted an `(all)` row, and reported `"matching_time_ns": 7` and `"matching_pct": 100.0`. Changing only `filterz` to the documented `filters` made both emit `"slices": []`, `"matching_time_ns": 0`, and `"matching_pct": 0.0`. The loaders at clankerprof/cli.py:162-172 and main.rs:906-929 return the complete mapping, while run_slices reads only recognized keys without rejecting the remainder.

findings.title: Rust report accepts an empty target config rejected by the target command
findings.claim: The single-pass Rust report bypasses the target command's nonempty-config validation, contradicting the tested contract that report sections match their individual subcommands.
findings.reproduction: With the valid 7ns facts artifact, `target/debug/clankerprof-rs report --facts ... --config <(printf '%s' '{}')` exited 0 and emitted `{"targets":{"parents":{},"tool":"clankerprof_targets"},"tool":"clankerprof_report"}`. Running `target/debug/clankerprof-rs targets` with the identical facts and config exited 2 with `{"error": "--config or --target is required.", "ok": false}`. run_report parses and analyzes directly at main.rs:439-452, omitting the `config.is_empty()` check used at main.rs:688-690. The report parity test only exercises a nonempty config.

findings.title: Rust report skips --top validation when no scope section is requested
findings.claim: Although report exposes `--top` as an integer-valued option, its value is parsed only inside the `--scopes-config` branch. A malformed supplied value therefore succeeds in target-only or slice-only reports.
findings.reproduction: Ran a target-only report with the valid 7ns facts artifact, nonempty target config `{"Leaf":{"kind":"runtime"}}`, and `--top junk`. `target/debug/clankerprof-rs report ... --top junk` exited 0 and emitted a normal `clankerprof_report` containing the 7ns target result. ReportArgs accepts the raw string at main.rs:272-274, but `int64_flag` is called only inside the scopes branch at main.rs:465-472. This contradicts SPEC.md:858-864, which requires every integer-valued CLI flag to reject non-integer grammar.

findings.title: Both protobuf decoders accept field numbers above the legal maximum
findings.claim: read_key rejects field zero but never enforces protobuf's maximum field number of 2^29-1, so malformed tags above the 29-bit domain are silently skipped.
findings.reproduction: The hand-built bytes `80 80 80 80 10 00` encode key 2^32 (field number 2^29, one above the maximum) followed by a zero varint. Both `.venv/bin/clankerprof facts --profile <(printf '\x80\x80\x80\x80\x10\x00')` and the equivalent `target/debug/clankerprof-rs` command exited 0 and emitted the identical empty facts artifact beginning `{"frames":[],"profile":...` with `"sample_count":0` and `"tool":"clankerprof_facts"`, rather than a decode error. Python checks only `field == 0` at proto.py:61-67; Rust does the same at proto.rs:82-91.

summary: Five new reproduced findings: three major false-success/configuration failures and two minor validation gaps. No unreproduced suspicions are included. The mandated gates could not run in the read-only audit sandbox: `./bin/dev check` failed immediately because `mktemp` was denied, and `cargo test --workspace` could not open `target/debug/.cargo-build-lock`; these environmental failures were not counted as findings. All probes were read-only against HEAD 9e57af8.
